headline: Build a Money-Making Website in 60 Seconds Using Your Voice
subheadline: Speak once. Launch instantly. Start earning.

heroPanel.heading: A live voice-to-website engine sits here
heroPanel.body: Generate a scrollable homepage preview, inspect the offer, and reserve the underlying source bundle without leaving the public site.

points.label: Instant preview
points.value: Client-side rendering makes the experience fast and tactile.

points.label: Voice capture
points.value: Speak your brief or type a conversion-focused offer.

points.label: Source bundle
points.value: Reserve the generated website package for fast checkout.

media.title: Watch the builder and source delivery flow in motion
media.body: The public site now uses motion and visual polish to make the product feel real and high-value.

workflowHeadline: A successful customer flow should feel simple from the outside and powerful underneath.

workflowSteps.title: Speak the idea
workflowSteps.description: Share the website goal in your own words, from the audience to the offer and the desired action.

workflowSteps.title: Generate the preview
workflowSteps.description: The engine turns that intent into a visual homepage concept, complete with hero media and an offer lane.

workflowSteps.title: Reserve the source
workflowSteps.description: Buy a source bundle or open the app store and use the generated preview as a product-ready starting point.

audienceIntro: The homepage now speaks directly to the buyers who want speed, clarity, and a usable launch asset.

audiences.title: Founders
audiences.description: Get a launch-ready page faster without hiring a full web team.
bullets: Convert ideas into a visible homepage instantly
Keep the experience focused on offer and action
Reserve a source bundle for quick deployment

audiences.title: Agencies
audiences.description: Standardize delivery with a preview-first intake flow and sell source packs confidently.
bullets: Use preview builds to close more proposals
Offer a tangible source package to clients
Save time on initial page design and copy

audiences.title: Technical teams
audiences.description: Manage generated assets, approvals, and checkout-ready delivery from one system.
bullets: Keep generated builds in a repository-friendly format
Support self-hosted and governed launch paths
Reduce manual handoffs between marketing and engineering

proofHeadline: The site should make the business model obvious.

proofCards.title: Subscription software
proofCards.description: Use the platform itself as the recurring core offer for founders and teams that need AI-managed delivery.
proofCards.outcome: Monthly revenue with expansion into seats, usage, and support.

proofCards.title: Launch and automation sprints
proofCards.description: Sell implementation packages to help customers set up workflows, pages, automations, and internal operating systems.
proofCards.outcome: Higher-ticket service revenue while the product matures.

proofCards.title: Enterprise deployment
proofCards.description: Offer self-hosted deployments, security review, onboarding, and customization for teams that cannot rely on pure SaaS.

productsIntro: These are the clearest monetization paths for the current product and market position.
faqHeadline: Questions that matter before a buyer commits

faq.question: Is this a website builder or an operations platform?
faq.answer: It is best positioned as an AI system manager that can also generate and manage website assets. That creates a stronger business and a more differentiated offer.

faq.question: How does the site make money early?
faq.answer: Early revenue should come from launch sprints and implementation packages, with software subscriptions growing as the platform becomes more productized.

faq.question: Who is the best first customer?
faq.answer: The best first customers are founders, agencies, and technical operators who already understand the value of automation and are willing to pay for speed.

finalCta.heading: Generate a preview, open the app store, and start selling source packs.
finalCta.body: The homepage now pushes toward a clearer revenue path: low-friction builder purchases, higher-demand app-store products, and stronger upsells into implementation or subscription offers.
